item.title: 周末人们很少使用复杂的git命令，我有数据可以证明。 | People rarely use complicated git commands on the weekend, and I have the data to prove it.
item.description:
序言：我们一直在运行一个实验项目，以对 DEV 上好的常青指南进行分类。 如果你好奇的话，这里有一些初步的工作。 这是一个低调的实验，所以不要过多地阅读当前存在的内容。 但这与这篇文章的主题无关。

在跟踪项目的过程中，我一直根据我们组织事物的方式密切关注这些指南的流量——从 CSS 和 Git 主题开始，我注意到一些非常明显的事情：周末 git 流量比 CSS 下降的幅度更大。

周末 CSS 流量下降约 40-50%。 这并不完全令人惊讶 - 只是很有趣。 在周末，工作最有可能是独自一人——如果有的话，最有可能以一种非常简单的方式使用 git，并且分支最少。

请记住，这不是整体开发，它是 git 相对于另一个相当普遍的工具。 我认为将 git 作为其流程的一部分的人不太可能根本不在他们的周末项目中使用它——但我希望他们更有可能留在自己的舒适区和日常生活中，并且不必抬头git 命令几乎与在协作工作环境中一样频繁。

我知道 Git，这取决于您所说的“知道”是什么意思。

支线快乐❤️



原文:

Preamble: We've been running an experimental project to catalog good evergreen guides on DEV. Some initial work is available here if you're curious. It's a low-key experiment so don't read too much into what's currently there. But that's an aside to the topic of this post.

In tracking the project, I've been keeping an eye on traffic coming to these guides based on how we organize things — and by starting with CSS and Git topics, I noticed something pretty stark: Weekend git traffic falls off wayyyyy more than CSS.

CSS traffic falls by about 40-50% on weekends. Git search traffic falls by about 80%. 🤯

This isn't exactly surprising — it's just interesting to see. On the weekend, work is most likely to be solo — and use of git, if at all, is most likely in a pretty straightforward way with minimal branching.

Remember, this is not overall development, it is git relative to another pretty ubiquitous tool. I think it's unlikely people who have git as part of their flow at all don't use it on their weekend projects — but I expect that they are way more likely to stay within their comfort zone and routine, and do not have to look up git commands nearly as often as in a collaborative work environment.

I know Git, depending on what you mean by "know".

Happy branching ❤️
item.author: ben

item.title: 如何在 Node 和 React 应用程序中使用 Docker | How to use Docker in your Node and React Applications
item.description:
这些容器可以在任何安装了 Docker 的机器上运行，并且应用程序是相同的。 这是在多个系统上运行代码库克隆的好方法，您可以确定它们都将是相同的。

使用 Docker 时 CI/CD 工作流程和 DevOps 测试环境明显更好，Docker 本质上是一组可以共享的软件工具。 在本教程中，我们将学习如何在 Docker 容器中创建和运行 NodeJS Express 后端和 React 前端。

在 Docker 中运行 NodeJS Express 后端

在开始之前，请确保您已在计算机上安装并运行 Docker。

现在使用命令行导航到类似桌面的目录，然后运行以下命令。

 mkdir my-app-docker cd my-app-docker touch docker-compose.yml mkdir api cd api npm init -y npm i express touch app.js Dockerfile .dockerignore cd ..

我们设置了一个名为 api 的后端并创建了一些 Docker 文件。 现在在代码编辑器中打开项目并将下面的代码添加到相应的文件中。

把它放在docker-compose.yml文件中。 请注意 yaml 格式，否则在尝试运行时会出现 Docker 错误。

 version : ' 3.8' services : api : build : ./api container_name : api_backend ports : - ' 4000:4000' volumes : - ./api:/app - ./app/node_modules

添加这是app.js文件。

 const express = require ( ' express ' ); const app = express (); const port = process . env . PORT || 4000 ; app . get ( ' / ' , ( req , res ) => { res . send ( ' Home Route ' ); }); app . listen ( port , () => console . log ( `Server running on port ${ port } , http://localhost: ${ port } ` ) );

现在将此行添加到.dockerignore文件中。

接下来将此代码添加到Dockerfile文件中。

 FROM node:16-alpine WORKDIR /app COPY package.json . RUN npm install COPY . . EXPOSE 4000 CMD [ "node" , "app.js" ]

最后将此运行脚本添加到package.json文件中。

 "scripts" : { "start" : "node app.js" } ,

我们刚刚创建了一个在端口 4000 上运行的基本 NodeJS Express 应用程序。该端口也映射到 docker 中的 4000，这让我们可以在 Docker 容器中运行它。

启动服务器

要像往常一样使用 Node 在 Docker 容器之外运行服务器，只需在命令行中运行以下代码。 您需要确保您位于 api 文件夹中。 如果您访问http://localhost:4000 ，您应该会在浏览器窗口中看到主路由。

让 NodeJS Express 应用程序在 Docker 内部运行需要不同的命令。 首先，您需要位于docker-compose.yml文件所在的根文件夹中。 现在运行下面的命令，它应该在 Docker 容器内运行。

不要忘记先停止节点服务器的运行，因为您只能在端口 4000 上运行一个服务器。

如果您访问http://localhost:4000 ，您应该会在浏览器窗口中看到主路由。

您可以使用以下命令停止服务器，也可以转到 Docker 应用程序并停止容器运行。

在 Docker 中运行 React 前端

现在让我们创建一个 React 前端！ 使用命令行进入 my-app-docker 的根文件夹。 运行以下命令来设置项目。

 npx create-react-app client cd client touch .dockerignore Dockerfile

现在将下面的代码添加到相应的文件中。

将此行添加到.dockerignore文件中。

将此代码放入Dockerfile文件中。

 FROM node:17-alpine WORKDIR /app COPY package.json . RUN npm install COPY . . EXPOSE 3000 CMD [ "npm" , "start" ]

最后使用下面的代码更新根文件夹中docker-compose.yml 。 我们在底部添加了一个客户端部分，其中包含让 React 在 Docker 容器内运行的设置。 请注意 yaml 格式，否则在尝试运行时会出现 Docker 错误。

 version : ' 3.8' services : api : build : ./api container_name : api_backend ports : - ' 4000:4000' volumes : - ./api:/app - ./app/node_modules client : build : ./client container_name : client_frontend ports : - ' 3000:3000' volumes : - ./client:/app - ./app/node_modules stdin_open : true tty : true

启动服务器

要像往常一样使用 Node 在 Docker 容器之外运行服务器，只需在命令行中运行以下代码。 确保您位于客户端文件夹中。 如果您访问http://localhost:3000 ，您应该会在浏览器窗口中看到主路由。

让 React 应用程序在 Docker 内部运行需要不同的命令。 首先，您需要位于docker-compose.yml文件所在的根文件夹中。 现在运行下面的命令，它应该在 Docker 容器内运行。

不要忘记先停止运行 React 应用服务器，因为您只能在端口 3000 上运行一个服务器。

如果您访问http://localhost:3000 ，您应该会在浏览器窗口中看到主路由。

您可以使用以下命令停止服务器，也可以转到 Docker 应用程序并停止容器运行。

通过此设置，您可以在 Docker 中同时运行 NodeJS 后端和 React 前端！ 如果您遇到任何错误，那么您可能需要打开 Docker 桌面应用程序并删除与此项目相关的所有图像。 然后您可以尝试再次运行docker-compose up命令，希望这次一切都应该按预期工作。



原文:

Docker gives developers the ability to package all of their applications inside of containers. These containers can run on any machine that has Docker installed and the application will be identical. This is a great way to run a clone of a codebase on multiple systems and you can be sure that they are all going to be the same.

CI/CD workflows and DevOps testing environments are significantly better when using Docker which is essentially a set of software tools that can be shared. Kubernetes is another tool which is used for operating multiple Docker containers but in a much larger scale.

In this tutorial we will learn how to create and run a NodeJS Express backend and a React frontend inside of a Docker container.

Running a NodeJS Express backend inside Docker

Before you begin make sure that you have Docker installed and running on your computer.

Now use the command line to navigate to a directory like your desktop then run the commands below.

mkdir my-app-docker
cd my-app-docker
touch docker-compose.yml
mkdir api
cd api
npm init -y
npm i express
touch app.js Dockerfile .dockerignore
cd ..

We setup a backend called api and created some Docker files. Now open the project in your code editor and add the code below to their corresponding files.

Put this in the docker-compose.yml file. Be careful with the yaml formatting otherwise you will get Docker errors when you try to run it.

version: '3.8'
services: api: build: ./api container_name: api_backend ports: - '4000:4000' volumes: - ./api:/app - ./app/node_modules

Add this is the app.js file.

const express = require('express'); const app = express(); const port = process.env.PORT || 4000; app.get('/', (req, res) => { res.send('Home Route');
}); app.listen(port, () => console.log(`Server running on port ${port}, http://localhost:${port}`)
);

Now add this line to the .dockerignore file.

Next add this code to the Dockerfile file.

FROM node:16-alpine WORKDIR /app COPY package.json . RUN npm install COPY . . EXPOSE 4000 CMD ["node", "app.js"]

Lastly add this run script to the package.json file.

"scripts": { "start": "node app.js" },

We just created a basic NodeJS Express app that runs on port 4000. That port is also mapped to 4000 in docker which lets us run it in a Docker container.

Starting the servers

To run the server outside of a Docker container using Node like normal just run the code below in your command line. You need to be sure that you are inside of the api folder. If you go to http://localhost:4000 you should see the home route in your browser window.

Getting the NodeJS Express app to run inside of Docker requires a different command. First you need to be in the root folder where the docker-compose.yml file is. Now run the command below and it should run inside of a Docker container.

Don't forget to stop the node server running first because you can only have one server running on port 4000.

If you go to http://localhost:4000 you should see the home route in your browser window.

You can stop the server with the command below or you can go to the Docker app and stop the container from running.

Running a React frontend inside Docker

Now lets create a React frontend! Use your command line to get inside of the root folder for my-app-docker. Run the commands below to setup the project.

npx create-react-app client
cd client
touch .dockerignore Dockerfile

Now add the code below into their corresponding files.

Add this line into the .dockerignore file.

Put this code into the Dockerfile file.

FROM node:17-alpine WORKDIR /app COPY package.json . RUN npm install COPY . . EXPOSE 3000 CMD ["npm", "start"]

Finally update the docker-compose.yml in the root folder with the code below. We have added a client section at the bottom with settings for getting React running inside of a Docker container. Be careful with the yaml formatting otherwise you will get Docker errors when you try to run it.

version: '3.8'
services: api: build: ./api container_name: api_backend ports: - '4000:4000' volumes: - ./api:/app - ./app/node_modules client: build: ./client container_name: client_frontend ports: - '3000:3000' volumes: - ./client:/app - ./app/node_modules stdin_open: true tty: true

Starting the servers

To run the server outside of a Docker container using Node like normal just run the code below in your command line. Make sure that you are inside of the client folder. If you go to http://localhost:3000 you should see the home route in your browser window.

Getting the React app to run inside of Docker requires a different command. First you need to be in the root folder where the docker-compose.yml file is. Now run the command below and it should run inside of a Docker container.

Don't forget to stop the React app server running first because you can only have one server running on port 3000.

If you go to http://localhost:3000 you should see the home route in your browser window.

You can stop the server with the command below or you can go to the Docker app and stop the container from running.

With this setup you can have a NodeJS backend and React frontend running at the same time inside of Docker! If you encounter any errors then you might need to open your Docker desktop application and remove any images that are related to this project. Then you can try running the docker-compose up command again and hopefully this time everything should be working as expected.
item.author: andrewbaisden

item.title: 编写 SQL 查询的第二次演练 | A Second Walk Through of Composing a SQL Query
item.description:
这建立在我的使用 Postgresql 和 Blazer 生成队列报告的演练中的工作之上。

我将构建的查询有助于回答活跃用户对至少一篇欢迎文章发表评论的百分比。 对于此查询，活跃用户是过去 7 天中第 4 天访问过网站的人。

建立实体关系模型

同样，在编写SQL时，我喜欢从关系图开始。 在这个数据分析中，我们有四个相关的表格。

 class User has_many :articles has_many :comments end class Article belongs_to :user has_many :comments , as: :commentable end class Comment belongs_to :commentable , polymorphic: true belongs_to :user end class PageView belongs_to :article belongs_to :user , optional: true end

下图是为那些更喜欢四种数据模型的实体关系模型 ( ERM ) 的人准备的图表。

这些查询中使用的表的 ERM。

查询所有活跃成员

我希望所有在过去 7 天中至少有 4 天拥有page_views的用户； 我们将考虑这些“活跃用户”。

首先，我想构建一个非常狭窄的查询； 一个让我确保我知道我在正确的道路上的方法。 我会将页面浏览量限制为我的user_id ：

 SELECT user_id , extract ( isodow from created_at ) AS day_of_week FROM page_views WHERE page_views . user_id = 702612 AND page_views . created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 GROUP BY page_views . user_id , day_of_week

以下查询超时； 它试图查询所有用户。

 SELECT dow . user_id , count ( dow . day_of_week ) AS number_of_days FROM ( SELECT user_id , extract ( isodow from created_at ) AS day_of_week FROM page_views WHERE page_views . created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 AND user_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY page_views . user_id , day_of_week ) AS dow GROUP BY dow . user_id HAVING count ( dow . day_of_week ) >= 4

由于页面浏览量巨大，我们需要仅限于最近更新的用户。 以下是获取最近用户的查询。

 SELECT id FROM users WHERE updated_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7

以下查询是基本的“谁是 Forem 的当前活跃用户”。

 SELECT DISTINCT dow . user_id FROM ( SELECT users . id AS user_id , extract ( isodow from page_views . created_at ) AS day_of_week FROM users INNER JOIN page_views ON page_views . user_id = users . id AND page_views . created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 AND user_id IS NOT NULL -- Extend the window for users just a bit to account -- -- for timing variance -- WHERE users . updated_at > NOW ():: date - INTERVAL '8 day' GROUP BY users . id , day_of_week ) AS dow GROUP BY dow . user_id HAVING count ( dow . day_of_week ) >= 4

我将上述查询“保存”到https://dev.to/admin/blazer/queries/717-regular-and-active-recent-users-of-dev 。 我们现在有了“谁是 DEV.to 的当前活跃用户”查询。

查询对欢迎帖发表评论的活跃用户

下一部分是确定谁对欢迎帖子发表了评论。 在使用 Postgresql 和 Blazer 生成队列报告的演练中，我写了关于查找对欢迎文章发表评论的用户。

但是，我需要调整同期群查询； 我只想要对欢迎帖子发表评论的用户。 同类群组查询包含对欢迎帖子发表评论和未发表评论的用户。

快速提醒一下，以下查询的结果是所有对欢迎帖子发表评论的user_id ； 但对用户的updated_at有限制

SELECT DISTINCT comments . user_id AS user_id FROM comments INNER JOIN users ON comments . user_id = users . id -- Extend the window for users just a bit to -- -- account for timing variance -- AND users . updated_at > CURENT_DATE - 8 INNER JOIN articles ON comments . commentable_id = articles . id AND comments . commentable_type = 'Article' AND articles . title LIKE 'Welcome Thread - v%' AND articles . published = true AND articles . user_id = 3 GROUP BY comments . user_id

现在合并这两个查询。 我正在使用 Postgresql WITH语句创建两个查询，我可以稍后引用。 我发现WITH语句有助于“封装”查询，并希望使它们在概念上更易于理解。

 WITH cow AS ( -- User IDs of recent folks who have commented on the -- welcome threads -- SELECT DISTINCT comments . user_id AS user_id FROM comments INNER JOIN users ON comments . user_id = users . id -- Extend the window for users just a bit to account -- -- for timing variance -- AND users . updated_at > CURENT_DATE - 8 INNER JOIN articles ON comments . commentable_id = articles . id AND comments . commentable_type = 'Article' AND articles . title LIKE 'Welcome Thread - v%' AND articles . published = true AND articles . user_id = 3 GROUP BY comments . user_id ), dow AS ( -- User IDs of folks who have interacted at least 4 different -- days of this week -- SELECT user_id FROM ( SELECT users . id AS user_id , extract ( isodow from page_views . created_at ) AS day_of_week FROM users INNER JOIN page_views ON page_views . user_id = users . id AND page_views . created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 AND user_id IS NOT NULL -- Extend the window for users just a bit to account for -- timing variance -- WHERE users . updated_at > CURRENT_DATE - 8 GROUP BY users . id , day_of_week ) AS dows GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT ( day_of_week ) >= 4 ) SELECT COUNT ( dow . user_id ) AS count_of_users , ( SELECT COUNT ( * ) FROM dow INNER JOIN cow ON cow . user_id = dow . user_id ) AS count_of_users_that_said_hello FROM dow

结论

当我编写最后一个查询时，我不断得到一个结果，表明网站上的每个活跃用户都评论了一篇欢迎文章。 我不相信这个结果。 这似乎不太可能。 我重新审视了我的查询和逻辑，发现了我的错误，重新编写了查询并得到了更合理的答案。

什么问题？ 我已经从Walk Through of Using Postgresql and Blazer to Generate a Cohort Report中复制并粘贴了一个查询。 但这个问题不是正确的问题。 它确实突出了SQL的一项挑战； 很难测试查询的正确性。



原文:

This builds on work from my Walk Through of Using Postgresql and Blazer to Generate a Cohort Report.

The query I’ll be building helps answer what percentage of active users commented on at least one welcome article. For this query, an active user is someone who’s been on the site 4 of the last 7 days.

Establishing the Entity Relationship Model

Again, when writing SQL, I like to start with a relationship diagram. In this data foray, we have four relevant tables.

class User has_many :articles has_many :comments
end class Article belongs_to :user has_many :comments, as: :commentable
end class Comment belongs_to :commentable, polymorphic: true belongs_to :user
end class PageView belongs_to :article belongs_to :user, optional: true
end

Below is a diagram for those of you who prefer an Entity Relationship Model (ERM) of the four data models.

An ERM of the tables used in these queries.

Querying All Active Members

I want all users who have page_views on at least 4 of the last seven days; we’ll consider these “active users.”

First I want to build a very narrow query; one that lets me make sure I know I’m on the right path. I’ll limit the page views to my user_id:

SELECT user_id, extract(isodow from created_at) AS day_of_week
FROM page_views
WHERE page_views.user_id = 702612 AND page_views.created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7
GROUP BY page_views.user_id, day_of_week

The following query times out; It’s trying to query all users.

SELECT dow.user_id, count(dow.day_of_week) AS number_of_days
FROM ( SELECT user_id, extract(isodow from created_at) AS day_of_week FROM page_views WHERE page_views.created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 AND user_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY page_views.user_id, day_of_week
) AS dow
GROUP BY dow.user_id
HAVING count(dow.day_of_week) >= 4

Because of the enormity of the page views we need to limit to only recently updated users. The following is the query to get recent users.

SELECT id
FROM users
WHERE updated_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7

The following query is the foundational “Who are the current active users of Forem.”

SELECT DISTINCT dow.user_id FROM ( SELECT users.id AS user_id, extract(isodow from page_views.created_at) AS day_of_week FROM users INNER JOIN page_views ON page_views.user_id = users.id AND page_views.created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 AND user_id IS NOT NULL -- Extend the window for users just a bit to account -- -- for timing variance -- WHERE users.updated_at > NOW()::date - INTERVAL '8 day' GROUP BY users.id, day_of_week) AS dow
GROUP BY dow.user_id
HAVING count(dow.day_of_week) >= 4

I “saved” the above query to https://dev.to/admin/blazer/queries/717-regular-and-active-recent-users-of-dev. We now have our “who’s the currently active users of DEV.to” query.

Querying Active Users Who Have Commented on a Welcome Post

The next part is to work out who all commented on a welcome post. In Walk Through of Using Postgresql and Blazer to Generate a Cohort Report, I wrote about finding the users who had commented on the welcome article.

However, I need to adjust the cohort query; I only want users who commented on the welcome post. The cohort query has users who did and did not comment on the welcome post.

As a quick reminder, the result of the following query is all user_id that commented on a welcome post; but with a limitation on the user’s updated_at

SELECT DISTINCT comments.user_id AS user_id
FROM comments
INNER JOIN users ON comments.user_id = users.id -- Extend the window for users just a bit to -- -- account for timing variance -- AND users.updated_at > CURENT_DATE - 8
INNER JOIN articles ON comments.commentable_id = articles.id AND comments.commentable_type = 'Article' AND articles.title LIKE 'Welcome Thread - v%' AND articles.published = true AND articles.user_id = 3
GROUP BY comments.user_id

Now to meld the two queries. I’m using the Postgresql WITH statement to create two queries that I can reference later on. I find the WITH statement to help “encapsulate” queries and hopefully make them more conceptually understandable.

WITH cow AS ( -- User IDs of recent folks who have commented on the -- welcome threads -- SELECT DISTINCT comments.user_id AS user_id FROM comments INNER JOIN users ON comments.user_id = users.id -- Extend the window for users just a bit to account -- -- for timing variance -- AND users.updated_at > CURENT_DATE - 8 INNER JOIN articles ON comments.commentable_id = articles.id AND comments.commentable_type = 'Article' AND articles.title LIKE 'Welcome Thread - v%' AND articles.published = true AND articles.user_id = 3 GROUP BY comments.user_id
), dow AS ( -- User IDs of folks who have interacted at least 4 different -- days of this week -- SELECT user_id FROM ( SELECT users.id AS user_id, extract(isodow from page_views.created_at) AS day_of_week FROM users INNER JOIN page_views ON page_views.user_id = users.id AND page_views.created_at > CURRENT_DATE - 7 AND user_id IS NOT NULL -- Extend the window for users just a bit to account for -- timing variance -- WHERE users.updated_at > CURRENT_DATE - 8 GROUP BY users.id, day_of_week ) AS dows GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(day_of_week) >= 4
) SELECT COUNT(dow.user_id) AS count_of_users, ( SELECT COUNT(*) FROM dow INNER JOIN cow ON cow.user_id = dow.user_id ) AS count_of_users_that_said_hello
FROM dow

Conclusion

When I was writing the last query, I kept getting a result that said every active user on the site had commented on a welcome article. I didn’t trust that result; it seemed highly improbable. I revisited my queries and logic, found my error, reworked the query and got a more reasonable answer.

What was wrong? I had copied and pasted a query from Walk Through of Using Postgresql and Blazer to Generate a Cohort Report. But that query wasn’t the right thing to ask. It does highlight one challenge of SQL; it can be hard to test the correctness of your query.

item.title: 谁能推荐一个 Ruby 好东西的小工具箱？ | Can anyone recommend a little toolbox of Ruby goodies?
item.description:
更具体地说，秘密藏匿：

  • 脚本
  • 工具
  • 实用程序
  • 可重用的类和方法（用于可重复的东西，如网页抓取、文件读取和写入）



原文:

More specifically a secret stash of:

  • Scripts
  • Tools
  • Utilities
  • Re-usable classes and methods (for repeatable stuff like web scraping, file read and writing)
item.author: leewynne

item.title: 设计可扩展且安全的 REST API 网关 | Designing a Scalable and Secure REST API Gateway
item.description:
微服务是目前软件开发领域的热门话题。 许多组织正在寻求采用这种架构风格，以提高他们快速有效地交付软件的能力。 微服务的主要挑战之一是以可扩展和可维护的方式设计整个系统。 在本文中，我们将讨论构建微服务时可以使用的三种流行设计模式：API 网关、前端后端和事件驱动架构。

它负责将请求路由到适当的后端服务，还可以提供额外的功能，例如负载平衡、缓存和安全性。 这使得它很容易上手，并且在规模上非常具有成本效益。 后端换前端

这使您可以针对每种类型的客户端优化后端服务，而无需复制任何逻辑。 重要的是要保持 BFF 精简，并且只包含绝对必要的逻辑。 另一个潜在的问题是，如果 BFF 设计不当，它们可能会在前端和后端之间引入紧密耦合。 重要的是设计您的 BFF，以便在需要时轻松更换或移除它们。

事件驱动架构

事件驱动架构 (EDA) 是一种设计模式，其中事件用于触发应用程序内的操作或工作流。 这允许系统的不同部分相互分离，并在需要进行更改时提供更大的灵活性。 当一起使用时，这些模式可以提供一种非常强大的方式来构建可扩展和可维护的应用程序。

如果您想开始使用微服务，那么您绝对应该查看这些设计模式。 他们将帮助您构建一个可以处理大量流量的强大且可扩展的系统。 如果您已经在使用微服务，那么这些模式将帮助您提高系统性能。



原文:

Microservices are a hot topic in the software development world right now. Many organizations are looking to adopt this architecture style in order to improve their ability to deliver software quickly and efficiently. One of the key challenges with microservices is designing the overall system in a way that is scalable and maintainable. In this article, we will discuss three popular design patterns that can be used when building microservices: API Gateway, Backend for Frontend, and Event-Driven Architecture.

API Gateway

An API Gateway is a microservice that acts as an entry point for all incoming requests. It is responsible for routing requests to the appropriate backend service, and can also provide additional functionality such as load balancing, caching, and security. The API Gateway pattern is often used in conjunction with the Microservice Pattern to provide a single point of access for all of your microservices.
AWS Lambda is a popular choice for implementing an API Gateway. Lambda is a serverless platform that allows you to run code without provisioning or managing any servers. This makes it very easy to get started with and can be very cost-effective at scale. Lambda also has built-in support for many common API Gateway use cases such as authentication, authorization, and rate limiting.

Backend For Frontend

The Backend For Frontend (BFF) pattern is a variation of the API Gateway pattern that is specifically designed to address the needs of mobile and web applications. The BFF sits between the client application and the backend services, and provides a tailored interface for each type of client. This allows you to optimize your backend services for each type of client without having to duplicate any logic. The BFF pattern can also help reduce the number of round trips between the client and backend by batching together related requests.
One common pitfall with BFFs is that they can start to become bloated over time as more and more client-specific logic is added to them. It is important to keep the BFFs lean and only include logic that is absolutely necessary. Another potential issue is that BFFs can introduce tight coupling between the frontend and backend if they are not designed properly. It is important to design your BFFs in a way that allows them to be easily replaced or removed if needed.

Event-Driven Architecture

Event-Driven Architecture (EDA) is a design pattern in which events are used to trigger actions or workflow within an application. This allows different parts of the system to be decoupled from each other and allows for more flexibility when changes need to be made. EDA can be used in conjunction with other design patterns such as Microservices or API Gateway. When used together, these patterns can provide a very powerful way to build scalable and maintainable applications.

If you're looking to get started with microservices, then you should definitely check out these design patterns. They'll help you build a robust and scalable system that can handle a lot of traffic. And if you're already using microservices, then these patterns will help you improve your system's performance.
item.author: roy8

item.title: 扑热息痛.test.js🧪| #4: Testea este código JavaScript #4：Testea este código JavaScript | Paracetamol.test.js🧪| #4: Testea este código JavaScript

 //arr.test.js const numPorDos = ( arr ) => { return arr . map (( item ) => { return item * 2 }); } test ( ' [1,2,3] => [2,4,6] ' , () => { const res = numPorDos ([ 1 , 2 , 3 ]); expect ( res ). toEqual ([ 2 , 4 , 6 ]); });

A. ❌ FAIL tests, Expected: [2,4,6] , Received: [1,2,3] A. ❌ FAIL tests, Expected: [2,4,6] , Received: [1,2,3]
B. ✅ PASS tests, [1,2,3] => [2,4,6] B. ✅ PASS tests, [1,2,3] => [2,4,6]

原文:

Cristian Fernando

Posted on Jun 15 

//arr.test.js
const numPorDos = (arr) => { return arr.map((item) => { return item * 2 });
} test('[1,2,3] => [2,4,6]', () => { const res = numPorDos([1,2,3]); expect(res).toEqual([2,4,6]);
});

A. ❌ FAIL tests, Expected: [2,4,6] , Received: [1,2,3]
B. ✅ PASS tests, [1,2,3] => [2,4,6]

item.author: duxtech

item.title: CSS卡片阴影效果 | CSS card shadow effects
item.description:
如今，卡片式组件仍然很流行，有很多方法可以让一堆相同的矩形看起来更漂亮。 我很喜欢波普艺术，或者它更像是漫画书风格的艺术指导，使用粗粗的轮廓和鲜艳的色彩。

所以我们想要的外观和感觉如下图所示：

较大阴影的设计细节如下（摘自 Figma）：

 /* Card */ height : 240 px ; width : 160 px ; left : 260 px ; top : 0 px ; border-radius : 8 px ; position : absolute ; width : 160 px ; height : 240 px ; background : #FFFFFF ; border : 4 px solid #1 F1F1F ; border-radius : 8 px ; /* Shadow */ height : 240 px ; width : 160 px ; left : 274 px ; /* Indicates shadow is offset on x-axis by 14px */ top : 14 px ; /* Indicates shadow is offset on y-axis by 14px */ border-radius : 8 px ; position : absolute ; width : 160 px ; height : 240 px ; background : #FFD700 ; border : 4 px solid #1 F1F1F ; border-radius : 8 px ;

概括地说，卡片有一个 4px 实心边框和 8px 边框半径，并且卡片的阴影也有一个实心 4px 边框和 8px 边框半径，并且在 x 和 y 轴上与卡片偏移 14px .

起初，我认为这会很容易，因为我之前使用过多个盒子阴影非常满意（参见下面的 Codepen，特别是前 3 张专辑上的点图案）：

我的想法是使用 2 个盒子阴影来创建彩色阴影和彩色阴影的边框。

但首先，让我们回顾一下box-shadow

长度值按特定顺序表示x-offset 、 y-offset 、 blur-radius和spread-radius 。

如果您不经常使用box-shadow ，这可能会让您检查文档：

  • 如果使用 2 个长度值，它们分别表示 x 偏移和 y 偏移，而其他 2 个值默认为 0。
  • 如果使用 3 个长度值，它们分别表示 x 偏移、y 偏移和模糊半径，而扩展半径默认为 0。
  • 如果您使用 4 个长度值，它们表示 x 偏移、y 偏移、模糊半径和扩展半径。

多个盒子阴影也是可能的，只要你用逗号分隔每个盒子阴影，如下所示：

 div { box-shadow : 4px 4px coral , 6px 6px olive , 8px 8px dodgerblue ; }

尝试 1：box-shadow 但不太正确

好的，所以我们正在尝试制作一个带有 4px 实心边框和 8px 边框半径的卡片，以及一个阴影，它也有一个实心 4px 边框和 8px 边框半径，并且在两个卡片上都偏移了 14px x 和 y 轴。

我的第一次尝试是这样的：

 .card { height : 300px ; width : 200px ; border : 4px solid black ; border-radius : 8px ; box-shadow : 18px 18px 0 0 gold , 18px 18px 0 4px black ; }

您可能已经注意到阴影上的边界半径与卡片的边界半径不匹配。 这是因为我为黑色边框添加的第二个阴影使用了 4px 扩展半径，将阴影扩大到卡片的原始尺寸之外。

现在，如果卡片上没有圆角，这根本不是问题，但它就是这样。 这种边界半径的差异有点太明显了，以至于这种方法无法接受。

尝试2：定位伪元素

进入下一次尝试！ 我的卡影套还有更多花样。 那么让我们使用伪元素方法。 要点是设置伪元素的样式，使其看起来与卡片完全一样，除了背景颜色不同且位于卡片下方。

 .card { position : relative ; background : white ; transform-style : preserve-3d ; } .card ::before { content : '' ; position : absolute ; height : 100% ; width : 100% ; border : 4px solid black ; background-color : gold ; bottom : -18px ; right : -18px ; border-radius : 8px ; transform : translateZ ( -1px ); box-sizing : content-box ; /* Only if you have some reset that makes everything border-box */ }

这里有趣的部分是卡片上的transform: translateZ(-1px) transform: translateZ(-1px)和伪元素阴影上的 transform: translateZ(-1px)。 这是为了确保卡片阴影始终呈现在卡片本身的后面。

我确实尝试只使用z-index: -1但不知何故它在 Codepen 中有效，但在上面的这个实时示例中无效。 我可能需要深入挖掘根本原因，但现在让我们使用转换技巧。

尝试 3：带插图的盒子阴影

我的一个同事觉得可以用 box-shadow 简化伪元素，当我告诉他我遇到的问题时，他提出了这个替代方案：

 .card { box-shadow : 14px 14px 0 -4px gold , 14px 14px 0 0 black ; }

由于我的问题是阴影边框超出了卡片的原始大小，这意味着边框应该是卡片阴影的“限制”。 它的传播半径应该是 0。这意味着我们应该为卡片阴影中所需的纯色使用负值。

瞧！ 问题解决了。 代码再次变得更短，一切都很好。

包起来

老实说，我根本没有想到要为 box-shadow 使用负值。 但是现在我知道了这种方法，我不能忽视它。 很高兴在已建立的 CSS 属性上发现新技术。 尤其是当这么多新事物即将出现时。

另外，我刚从2022 年 CSS 日回来，在现实生活中遇到我最喜欢的 CSS 人，这很鼓舞人心。 相信我，当我说我在会议的前 3 个小时里比自 COVID 开始和事情关闭以来受到的启发更大。

Anyhoo, I might have some mildly spicy opinions and ideas in the upcoming posts, so stay tuned if you feel like it, I guess? Anyhoo，我可能会在即将发布的帖子中提出一些温和的观点和想法，所以如果您愿意，请继续关注，我猜？ 🌶️ 🌶️



原文:

Card-style components are pretty much still a thing these days, and there are many ways to make a bunch of identical rectangles look prettier. I’m quite fond of the pop art, or maybe it’s more of a comic book style art direction that uses thick bold outlines and bright colours.

So the look and feel we are going for looks something like the image below:

Specifics of the design for the larger shadow are as follows (extracted from Figma):

/* Card */
height: 240px;
width: 160px;
left: 260px;
top: 0px;
border-radius: 8px;
position: absolute;
width: 160px;
height: 240px;
background: #FFFFFF;
border: 4px solid #1F1F1F;
border-radius: 8px; /* Shadow */
height: 240px;
width: 160px;
left: 274px; /* Indicates shadow is offset on x-axis by 14px */
top: 14px; /* Indicates shadow is offset on y-axis by 14px */
border-radius: 8px;
position: absolute;
width: 160px;
height: 240px;
background: #FFD700;
border: 4px solid #1F1F1F;
border-radius: 8px;

To summarise in words, the card has a solid 4px border and a border-radius of 8px, and the card’s shadow too has a solid 4px border with 8px border-radius and is offset from the card by 14px on both the x and y axes.

At first, I thought this would be quite easy, because I had used multiple box shadows before to great satisfaction (see Codepen below, specifically the pattern of dots on the first 3 albums):

My idea was to use 2 box shadows to create the coloured shadow and the border for the coloured shadow.

But first, let’s review box-shadow

The full syntax for the box-shadow property can take 2-4 length values, an optional colour and an optional inset keyword. The length values represent the x-offset, the y-offset, the blur-radius and the spread-radius, in that specific order.

Here’s the bit that will probably make you check the documentation if you do not use box-shadow often:

  • If you use 2 length values, they mean the x-offset and the y-offset respectively, while the other 2 values default to 0.
  • If you use 3 length values, they mean the x-offset, the y-offset and the blur-radius respectively, while the spread-radius defaults to 0.
  • If you use 4 length values, they mean the x-offset, the y-offset, the blur-radius and the spread-radius.

Multiple box shadows are also possible, as long as you separate each box shadow with a comma like so:

div { box-shadow: 4px 4px coral, 6px 6px olive, 8px 8px dodgerblue;
}

Attempt 1: box-shadow but not quite right

Okay, so we’re trying to do a card with a solid 4px border and a border-radius of 8px, and a shadow which also has a solid 4px border with 8px border-radius and is offset from the card by 14px on both the x and y axes.

My first attempt looked like this:

.card { height: 300px; width: 200px; border: 4px solid black; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 18px 18px 0 0 gold, 18px 18px 0 4px black;
}

You might have noticed that the border-radius on the shadow does not match that of the card. This is because the second shadow that I added for the black border which used a 4px spread-radius expanded the shadow beyond the original size of the card.

Now, if there were no rounded corners on the cards, this wouldn’t be an issue at all, but it is what it is. And this discrepancy in border-radius was a little too visible for this approach to be acceptable.

Attempt 2: positioned pseudo-element

Onto the next try! There are more tricks up my card shadow sleeve. Let’s go with the pseudo-element approach then. The gist is to style the pseudo-element to look exactly like the card except with a different background colour and positioned below the card.

.card { position: relative; background: white; transform-style: preserve-3d;
} .card::before { content: ''; position: absolute; height: 100%; width: 100%; border: 4px solid black; background-color: gold; bottom: -18px; right: -18px; border-radius: 8px; transform: translateZ(-1px); box-sizing: content-box; /* Only if you have some reset that makes everything border-box */
}

The interesting part here was the transform: translateZ(-1px) on the card and the transform: translateZ(-1px) on the pseudo-element shadow. This is to ensure that the card shadow always renders behind the card itself.

I did try using just z-index: -1 but somehow it worked in Codepen but not on this live example above. I probably need to dig into the root cause but let’s use the transform trick for now.

Attempt 3: box-shadow with inset

A colleague of mine felt that the pseudo-element could be simplified with box-shadow, and when I told him the issue I ran into, he proposed this alternative:

.card { box-shadow: 14px 14px 0 -4px gold, 14px 14px 0 0 black;
}

Since my problem was that the shadow border was growing beyond the original size of the card, that meant that the border should be the “limit” of the card shadow. Its spread radius should be 0. This meant that we ought to use a negative value for the solid colour needed in the card shadow.

And voila! Problem solved. Code became much shorter again, and all is well with the world.

Wrapping up

Honestly, it did not occur to me to use a negative value for the box-shadow at all. But now that I know about this approach, I cannot unsee it. It’s nice to discover new techniques on established CSS properties. Especially when so many new things are on the horizon.

Also, I just came back from CSS Day 2022 and boy was it inspiring to meet my favourite CSS folks in real life. Trust me when I say I was more inspired in the first 3 hours of the conference than I had been since COVID started and things shut down.

Anyhoo, I might have some mildly spicy opinions and ideas in the upcoming posts, so stay tuned if you feel like it, I guess? 🌶️
item.author: huijing

item.title: 谁负责辅助功能？ | Who is responsible for Accessibility?
item.description:
在成为软件工程师之前，我是一名装饰师。 我参加了一门建筑技能课程，其中一部分是为考试而学习以获得我的建筑技能认证计划 (CSCS) 卡； 在英国建筑工地工作所需的健康和安全认证。

自从我成为一名装饰师以来已经有一段时间了，我可能已经忘记了 CSCS 问题的大部分答案，但我记得的一件事是我的导师每天重复一个问题和答案：

问：谁负责健康和安全？
答：大家。

鉴于建筑工地的危险性，每个人都知道如何安全工作很重要。 提供正确的 PPE（个人防护设备，我相信自大流行以来我们都熟悉这个首字母缩写词......）、培训、程序和设备是雇主的责任，但这取决于现场的每个人使用和遵循他们也正确。

无论您在现场担任什么角色，您都要对自己和周围人的健康和安全负责。

随着我越来越多地参与 a11y（可访问性）的所有事情，我已经看到如何在这里应用相同的概念。

谁负责可访问性？

每个人。

当我第一次成为一名开发人员并开始学习可访问性时，我是从纯粹的开发人员角度考虑的——我是一名开发人员，所以我有责任让我的代码可访问。 随着我了解的更多，我认为它既是开发人员的职责，也是 QA 的职责。 毕竟，必须有人测试代码以确保它是可访问的。

当我在颜色、对比度和文本方面遇到 a11y 违规时，我开始从 UX 的角度思考它，在那个阶段，我知道它的范围远不止于此。

大家呢？ 真的吗？

是的！

我正在考虑我在我工作过的公司中遇到的角色，我想到了不同领域的多个例子。

营销和内容团队可以确保他们的内容对于具有不同理解和阅读能力水平的人来说清晰易懂。

人员团队可以通过以多种格式提供职位描述和其他重要信息，为残疾人制定包容性的招聘和入职流程。

这些只是我想到的几个例子，但我希望它说明每个人都可以如何参与，不仅是网站，还有公司更容易访问。

当我们考虑超越开发工作限制的可访问性时，我们可以取得更多成就，当我们为非技术人员开放空间加入时，我们会学到更多。最成功的可访问性项目是那些不限制开发人员参与的项目而是更真实地反映了组成许多团队的多个角色。

感谢您的阅读，让我知道您的想法在下面！



原文:

Before I became a Software Engineer, I worked as a decorator. I did a course in Construction Skills and part of that was studying for the test to get my Construction Skills Certification Scheme (CSCS) card; a Health and Safety certification required for working on construction sites in the UK.

It’s been quite a while since I was a decorator now and I’ve probably forgotten most of the answers to the CSCS questions but one thing I do remember is my tutor’s daily repetition of one question and answer:

Q: Who is responsible for health and safety?
A: Everyone.

Given how dangerous a construction site can be, it's important everyone knows how to work safely. It's the employer's responsibility to provide the correct PPE (Personal Protective Equipment, I'm sure we're all familiar with that acronym since the pandemic...), training, procedures and equipment but it's up to everyone on site to use and follow them correctly too.

No matter what your role is on site, you are responsible for the health and safety of yourself and those around you.

As I’ve become more involved in all things a11y (accessibility), I've seen how the same concept can be applied here.

Who is responsible for accessibility?

Everyone.

When I first became a developer and started learning about accessibility, I thought of it in purely dev terms - I was a developer so it’s my responsibility to make my code accessible. As I learned more, I thought about it as both a developer and a QA’s responsibility. After all, someone has to test the code to make sure it’s accessible.

When I encountered a11y violations with colours, contrast and text, I began thinking about it from a UX point of view and at that stage, I knew enough to realise it was much wider ranging than that.

Everyone, though? Really?

Yes!

I’m thinking about the roles that I’ve encountered in the companies I’ve worked for and multiple examples across different areas come to mind.

Marketing and Content teams can ensure their content is clear and easy to understand for people with different levels of comprehension and reading abilities.

BAs can help make sure accessibility requirements are included and advocated for in planned work.

People teams can make hiring and onboarding processes inclusive for disabled people by providing job descriptions and other important information in multiple formats.

These are just a few examples that occurred to me but I hope it illustrates how everyone can play a part in making, not just websites, but companies more accessible.

We can achieve much more when we think about accessibility beyond the limits of development work and we learn much more when we open up spaces for non-technical people to join in. The most successful accessibility programmes are those that don't limit involvement to developers but instead are a truer reflection of the multiple roles that make up many teams.

Thanks for reading, let me know what your thoughts are below!
item.author: horrorofpartybeach

item.title: 6 月 6 日针对 Web 开发人员的 UX 和 a11y 提示 | 6 June tips about UX and a11y for web devs
item.description:
嘿伙计！

我想谈谈为患有前庭障碍的人创建动画，改善重置密码和填写电话号码时的用户体验，编写屏幕阅读器搜索和显示密码模式。

但在开始阅读之前，我在我的 Substack 时事通讯上留下了关于 UX 和 a11y 技巧的链接。 你知道要做什么😎

我们走吧！

照顾可能出现头晕、恶心和头痛的用户

你知道你的动画可能会导致头晕、恶心和头痛吗？ 它描述了患有前庭疾病的用户在看到运动动画时会出现头晕、恶心和头痛😩

所以这不会发生，我们应该使用偏好减少运动媒体功能👀 患有前庭疾病的人可能会禁用操作系统选项中的动画。

而且我们可以检测到使用偏好减少运动媒体功能仅为没有障碍的人显示动画。 所以我们会更好的照顾用户😊

不要这样做

.example { animation-name : zoomInDown ; animation-duration : 1s ; animation-timing-function : ease-out ; animation-delay : 0.3s ; }

你可以改用这个

@media ( prefers-reduced-motion : no-preference ){ .example { animation-name : zoomInDown ; animation-duration : 1s ; animation-timing-function : ease-out ; animation-delay : 0.3s ; } }

只留下数字！

各位小伙伴们，在登录 Telegram 网站时，输入数字代码时，你会被 # 或 * 符号弄糊涂吗？ 就个人而言，我是的，因为我不看键盘就自动点击。 所以有时我输入 * 而不是数字😁

因此，我喜欢当应用程序显示带有数字但没有特殊符号的键盘时。 尤其是使用 inputmode HTML 属性可以很容易地完成☝️

表单验证后保存焦点

我敢肯定，当他通过表单验证时，专注可能会改善用户的生活😊

让我们看看 Preply。 如果我们犯了密码错误，应用程序会通知我们该错误。 但是......它也重置了用户焦点🤦‍♂️所以我必须用鼠标再次点击密码输入字段。 只有这样我才能纠正错误😒

我们能做些什么呢？ 我在Booking上找到了一个很好的例子。 如果我们尝试登录应用程序可以节省焦点！ 而且我没有采取更多行动，只能纠正错误。 耶！🙌

将国家代码与电话号码的主要部分分开

伙计们，几天前我在 Preply 上填写我的电话号码。 当我保存更改时，我犯了错误😒

当然我想修复它。 所以我开始编辑它，按 ctrl+A 并删除键。 哦......我看到国家代码被删除了......所以我不得不用+7（code my country）填写我的电话号码🤦‍♂️

那太糟糕了😤但我认为这可能是一个新建议。 所以我开始研究并在Airbnb上找到了一个很好的例子😍

当我们填写电话号码时，密钥代码是一个单独的部分。 因此，如果我们犯了错误，我们可以选择全部，删除并重新开始填写，而不需要代码国家。 减去一个动作。 惊人的！ 您想帮助有视力障碍的用户使用搜索吗？

伙计们，您知道视力障碍者是如何开始在应用程序中搜索的吗？ 有一个名为“搜索”的特殊搜索 WAI-ARIA 角色。 如果我们将它用于搜索屏幕阅读器将开始以“区域列表”模式显示它，供用户快速导航页面☝️

如果不这样做，用户将不得不手动查看。 这要困难得多.... 😐 所以，请添加 role="search" 在您的应用程序中搜索

请通过 Enter 显示密码

伙计们，几周前我尝试登录 Booking 应用程序，但我的女朋友让我分心了。

天哪，在那之后我忘记了我输入的内容。 所以我按下 Tab 以使用显示密码按钮。 但是焦点在登录按钮上移动时错过了它......这是不真实的失败🤦‍♂️

如何让它变得更好？ 查看爱彼迎网站。 它们允许用户使用键盘从字段快速聚焦到按钮。 按 T​​ab 然后 Enter，您会看到您的密码！ 干杯! 附言
请使用我的电子邮件进行交流👉 melnik909@ya.ru

💪 Get more free tips directly to your inbox 💪 将更多免费提示直接发送到您的收件箱

❤ Thank you so much, my sponsors: Ben Rinehart, Sergio Kagiema, Jesse Willard, Tanya Ten, Konstantinos Kapenekakis. ❤ 非常感谢，我的赞助商：Ben Rinehart、Sergio Kagiema、Jesse Willard、Tanya Ten、Konstantinos Kapenekakis。



原文:

Hey folks!

I'd like to talk about creating animation for people with vestibular disorders, improving user experience when resetting password and filling in phone number, coding a search for screen readers and the show password pattern.

But before embarking on reading I leave the link on my Substack newsletter about UX and a11y tips. You know what to make 😎

Let's go!

Taking care of users which can experience dizziness, nausea and headaches

Did you know your animations might cause dizziness, nausea and headache? 🤔

The WCAG 2.2 has the section 2.3.3. which describes that users with vestibular disorders experience dizziness, nausea and headaches when they see a motion animation 😩

So that doesn't happen we should use the prefers-reduced-motion media feature 👀 People with vestibular disorders might disable animations in OS options.

And we can detect that using the prefers-reduced-motion media feature to display animations for only people without disorders. So we will take care of users better 😊

don't do this

.example { animation-name: zoomInDown; animation-duration: 1s; animation-timing-function: ease-out; animation-delay: 0.3s;
}

you can use this instead

@media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference){ .example { animation-name: zoomInDown; animation-duration: 1s; animation-timing-function: ease-out; animation-delay: 0.3s; }
}

Leave just the numbers!

Folks, will you confused by # or * symbols when entering digital code when logging in on Telegram website? 🤔

Personally, I'm yes because I automatically tap without looking at a keyboard. So sometimes I was entering * instead of digits 😁

Therefore I love when apps shows a keyboard with the digits without special symbols. Especially it can be done really easy just using the inputmode HTML attribute ☝️

Save focus after form validation

I'm sure focus might improve a user life when he goes through form validation 😊

Let's look at Preply. If we make a password mistake the app informs us about the mistake. But ... It resets user focus also 🤦‍♂️ So I have to take my mouse and click by password input field again. And only then I can fix the mistake 😒

What can we make instead? I found a nice example on Booking. If we try to log in the app saves focus! And I haven't make more action and can just fix the mistake. Yay!🙌

Separate a country code from the main part of a phone number

Folks, a few days ago I was filling in my phone number on Preply. When I saved changes I've made the mistake 😒

Sure I wanted to fix it. So I started to edit that, push ctrl+A and delete keys. Oh... And I saw the country code was deleted.... So I had to fill in my phone number with +7 (code my country) 🤦‍♂️

That was terrible 😤 But I thought that may be a new advice. So I started to research and found the great example on Airbnb 😍

When we fill in a phone number a key code is a separate part. Thus if we made a mistake we can select all, delete and start to fill in it again without a code country. Minus one action. Awesome! 😎

Do you wanna help users with vision disabilities use search?

Folks, did you know how people with vision disabilities start searching in apps? 🤔

There is a special search WAI-ARIA role that's named 'search'. If we use it for search screen readers will start to display it in the "Regions list" mode that is used by users for quick navigation by page ☝️

If that isn't done, users will have to look by hand. It's a lot harder.... 😐 So, please, add role="search" to search in your apps 🙏

Please, show password via Enter

Folks, a few weeks ago I tried to log in to Booking app and my girlfriend distracted me.

Blimey, after that I forgot what I typed. So I pushed Tab to use the Show password button. But focus has missed it having moved on the Sign in button.... It's the unreal fail 🤦‍♂️

How to make that better? View the Airbnb website. They allow users to use the keyboard to quickly focus from the field to the button. Push Tab then Enter and you see your password! Cheers! 🥳

P.S.
✉️ I make free unlimited Q&A sessions about user-friendly web-interfaces (UX, a11y) via email with expect responses in 2 days.

😎 I make free UX and a11y reviews of your non-commercial projects and recommendations for improvement.

🧑‍🏫I help in searching high-quality content about interfaces

Please, use my email for communication 👉 melnik909@ya.ru

💪 Get more free tips directly to your inbox

❤ Thank you so much, my sponsors: Ben Rinehart, Sergio Kagiema, Jesse Willard, Tanya Ten, Konstantinos Kapenekakis.
item.author: melnik909

item.title: 未记录，未完成！ | Not Documented, not Done!
item.description:
可能是我作为 CTO 和工程负责人在 Jira 和 GitHub 上最常用的评论 🙄

我讨厌写它。 但与此同时，我绝对相信记录任务结果是您可以编写的最有价值的文档之一。

不记录任务结果会产生许多负面后果。 没有文档，不直接参与任务的人不会知道

  • 如果任务完成、中止、跳过？
  • 在哪里可以找到任务的结果？
  • 它与其他任务有何关系？
  • 等等

随着团队的发展，所有这些都将变得非常重要，并且将在同一时间与许多人合作完成多项任务。

它也将成为“无证行为”的宝库，因为存储库多年来可能会增长到数千个问题。

任何旧的错误报告都可以成为有关尝试修复错误的知识来源以及关于为什么错误“未修复”的决定。 或者它可能只是一张“关闭”的票，让您对发生的事情感到困惑，并想知道这是否与您当前的任务有关。

因此，我建议大家在关闭问题时添加简短评论，涵盖以下内容

  • 结果是什么？
  • 您可以添加任何指向结果、文档等的链接吗？
  • 是否有在单独的问题中跟踪的后续任务或开放点？

成为“文件警察”的最大挑战是让团队成员不会感到受到检查或冒犯。 我通常以 1:1 的方式解决这些紧张关系，确保这是关于文档而不是审查工作质量的问题。



原文:

"Not Documented, not Done!" is probably my most used comment in Jira & GitHub as a CTO and engineering leader 🙄

I hate writing it. But at the same time I'm absolutely convinced that documenting tasks results is amongst the most valuable documentation you can write.

Not documenting task results will have many negative consequences. Without Documentation, People not directly involved with the task will not know

  • if the task got finished, aborted, skipped?
  • where to find the results of the tasks?
  • how it relates to other tasks?
  • etc.

All of which is going to be very important to know as the team grows and will be collaborating with many people on multiple tasks at the same time.

It's also going to be a treasure trove of "undocumented behavior" as Repositories can grow to thousands of issues over the years.

Any old bug report, can either be a source of knowledge on what was tried to fix the bug and decisions on why a bug was "not fixed". Or it can just be a "closed" ticket, leaving you puzzled about what happened and wondering if that's somehow related to your current task.

Therefore I recommend everyone to add short comment covering the following whenever they close an Issue

  • What was the outcome?
  • Are there any links to results, documents etc you can add?
  • Are there follow up tasks or open points that are being tracked in separate issues?

Biggest challenge in being "documentation police" is making that team members are not feeling checked up on, or otherwise offended. I usually resolve these tensions in a 1:1, ensuring that this is about the documentation and not about reviewing the quality of work.
item.author: vsaw

item.title: 使用 react-wizardry 构建智能向导 | Build smart wizards with react-wizardry
item.description:
向导是按顺序从用户那里收集信息的绝佳方式。

在这篇文章中，我们将了解如何使用react-wizardry库构建一个多步骤向导。

让我们首先安装库开始。

 npm install react-wizardry

页面是使用react-wizardry构建多步骤表单的核心。

每个Page对象代表向导中的一个页面。 每个Page对象都有一个配置输入字段的fields数组。

让我们使用pages属性来构建向导

<Wizard pages={[ { title: "Page 1", fields: [ { label: "First Name", name: "firstName", type: "text", }, { label: "Last Name", name: "lastName", type: "text" }, ] }, { title: "Page 2", fields: [ { label: "Date of Birth", name: "dob", type: "date" }, ] }, { title: "Page 3", fields: [ { name: "salary", label: "Salary", type: "number" }, ] }, ]} />

该向导已配置为三个页面。

在第 1 页上，有两个字段：名字和姓氏。

出生日期是第 2 页上的唯一字段，而薪水是最后一页上的唯一字段。

请务必记住，页面是按照 pages 数组中指定的顺序呈现的。

让我们看看 UI 到目前为止的样子。

在构建多步骤表单时，在进行下一步之前收集一些强制性信息非常重要。 在 react-wizardry 中，这可以通过isRequired属性来实现。

要使某个字段成为必填字段，我们只需将您希望用户正确输入的字段的isRequired设置为 true。

让我们在 pages 集合中设置firstName字段为必填项。

 fields: [ { label: "First Name", name: "firstName", type: "text" isRequired: true, }, { label: "Last Name", name: "lastName", type: "text" } ]

必填字段标有特殊的红色星号图标，下一步按钮将被禁用。 仅当用户填写该字段的值时，才会启用下一个按钮。

当字段被标记为isRequired时，默认情况下会打开验证。 该组件在该字段旁边显示一个很好的错误消息，当用户解决问题时，这些消息就会消失。

该库对更改不同元素的颜色具有精细的支持。 查看文档以获取完整的详细信息。

这是一个带有深色主题的向导示例

结论

除了我们看到的概念之外， react-wizardry还提供了诸如 RTL、自定义验证消息和开箱即用的可自定义步进图标等功能。

查看文档以获取更多详细信息，并在此处探索所有代码和框示例。

如果你喜欢这篇文章，你可能也会喜欢我的其他开源项目。

在Twitter和Github上关注我。



原文:

Wizards are an excellent way to collect information from users in a sequential manner.

In this post, we'll look at how to build a multi-step wizard using the react-wizardry library.

react-wizardry is a brand new react library built with Typescript for building multi step forms with in built validations.

Let’s get started by installing the library first.

npm install react-wizardry

⚡Setting up the pages

Pages are central to building multi step forms with react-wizardry.

Each Page object represents a page in the wizard. Every Page object has a fields array that configures the input fields.

Lets use the pages prop to build the wizard

<Wizard
  pages={[
    {
      title: "Page 1",
      fields: [
        {
          label: "First Name",
          name: "firstName",
          type: "text",
        },
        {
          label: "Last Name",
          name: "lastName",
          type: "text"
        },
      ]
    },
    {
      title: "Page 2",
      fields: [
        {
          label: "Date of Birth",
          name: "dob",
          type: "date"
        },
      ]
    },
    {
      title: "Page 3",
      fields: [
        {
          name: "salary",
          label: "Salary",
          type: "number"
        },
      ]
    },
  ]}
/>

The wizard has been configured with three pages. 

On page 1, there are two fields: first name and last name.

Date of Birth is the only field on Page 2, and Salary is the only field on the final page. 

It's crucial to keep in mind that the pages are rendered in the order specified in the pages array.

Let's see how the UI looks so far.

🔠 Field Types

The type property allows you to define the type of input field.

11 different input types are supported by react-wizardy.

text, email, phone, number, password, textarea, select, checkbox, datetime, radio, and file are the supported input types.

⚠️ Marking a field as mandatory

When building multi-step forms, it is important to collect some mandatory information before proceeding to the next step. In react-wizardry, this can be achieved via the isRequired property.

To make a field mandatory, all we need to do is set isRequired to true for the field that you want the user to enter without fail.

Let's make the firstName field mandatory in our pages collection.

fields: [
  {
    label: "First Name",
    name: "firstName",
    type: "text"
    isRequired: true,
  },
  {
    label: "Last Name",
    name: "lastName",
    type: "text"
  }
]

Mandatory fields are marked with a special red asterisk icon and the next button will be disabled. The next button will become enabled only when the value for the field is filled by the user.

✅ Field validations

All supported input fields in react-wizardry already have in built validation. When a field is marked as isRequired, validations are turned on by default. The component shows a nice error message right next to the field, and the messages go away when the user fixes the problem.

🎨 Themes

The library has granular support for changing the colors of the different elements. Checkout the documentation for complete details.

Here is an example of the wizard with the dark theme

Conclusion

Apart from the concepts we looked at, react-wizardry offers features such as RTL, custom validation messages, and customizable stepper icons out of the box.

Check out the documentation for more details and explore all the codesandbox samples here.

⭐ Star this project on Github

If you liked this post, you might also like my other open source projects.

Follow me on Twitter, Github.
item.author: prabhuignoto

item.title: 基于属性的测试#4：统一随机生成和收缩 | Property-based testing #4: Unifying Random Generation and Shrinking
item.description:
这是关于基于属性的测试系列的第四篇文章。 它描述了如何重构原始 QuickCheck API，以便统一随机生成和收缩。 该系列的第一篇也是介绍性文章是它到底是什么？ 这篇文章的完整代码可以在GitHub上找到——特别是example.py和Integrated.py 。

在上一篇文章中，我们在基于属性的测试库中添加了收缩。 这工作得很好，但我们触及了一些不太理想的方面——特别是我们现在需要用户实现一个收缩函数并将其传递给for_all 。 除了给用户带来额外的负担外，如果生成和收缩功能没有对齐，它还会导致一些令人困惑的行为。


一些很好对齐的石头，而不是随机生成和收缩。 照片由 Riccardo Ginevri 在 Unsplash 上拍摄

让我们看一个人为的例子：

 prop_weird_shrink = for_all ( int_between ( - 20 , - 1 ), shrink_int , lambda i : i * i < 0 )

我们正在生成严格的负数，并检查负数的乘积是否为正数，但错误地翻转了大于号。

我们很容易找到这个错误，但是在收缩过程中发生了一件奇怪的事情：

 > test ( prop_weird_shrink ) Fail : at test 0 with arguments ( - 20 ,). Shrinking : found smaller arguments ( 0 ,) Shrinking : gave up - smallest arguments found ( 0 ,)

我们将 0 报告为失败的最小元素，但 0 甚至不是一个可能的生成值！ 换句话说，收缩候选不满足与随机生成器相同的不变量。

我们是否可以重构我们的 API，使其看起来和感觉像随机生成 API，但仍然可以缩小？ 即我们的用户可以写：

 prop_weird_shrink = for_all ( int_between ( - 20 , - 1 ), lambda i : i * i < 0 )

并免费缩小？ 当然，同时自动满足与随机生成相同的不变量。

答案是肯定的，我们可以。 让我们看看如何。

这不是很容易吗？

值得简要解释一下为什么我们不能只打包随机生成和收缩的接口，在它们上实现原始函数并收工：

 class Arbitrary ( Generic [ Value ]): def generate ( self ) -> Value : ... def shrink ( self , value : Value ) -> Iterable [ Value ]: ...

例如，这对于int_between实现非常简单——我们已经有了随机生成，我们可以很容易地调整shrink_int以采用low, high参数，并确保较小的候选者位于该范围内。 真正的问题出现在我们尝试实现map for shrink时。 我们需要支持map ，因为我们希望 random+shrink 的 API 与 random 相同，并且 random 支持map 。

我们想要一个带有这个签名的函数：

 def shrink_map ( f : Callable [[ T ], U ], s : Shrink [ T ]) -> Shrink [ U ]: def shrinker ( value : U ) -> Iterable [ U ]: ... return shrinker

请记住， Shrink[T]仅表示接受T并返回较小值的Iterable[T]的函数。 所以内部shrinker功能是shrink_map的结果。 现在如何实施呢？ 我们可以将f应用于输入收缩器s生成的每个候选者：

 def shrink_map ( f : Callable [[ T ], U ], s : Shrink [ T ]) -> Shrink [ U ]: def shrinker ( value : U ) -> Iterable [ U ]: for candidate in s (...): yield f ( candidate ) return shrinker

那肯定会给我们一堆U候选值。 但问题是s需要一些值T来产生它的候选者，而我们没有它！ 我们只有一个值U 。 为了说明这一点，这里有一个具体类型的示例——比如我想将shrink_int转换为shrink_char毕竟，有一个 ASCII 码整数到字符的映射。 简单，你说，只需使用ord将字符转换为它的 ASCII 码整数，然后使用chr转换回它的字符：

 def make_shrink_char ( shrink_int : Shrink [ int ]) -> Shrink [ str ]: def shrinker ( value : str ) -> Iterable [ str ]: for candidate in shrink_int ( ord ( value )): yield chr ( candidate ) return shrinker

但这不是一个映射——事实上，要将一个shrink_int转换为一个shrink_char ，我们需要两个函数：一个将整数转换为字符，一个将字符转换为整数。 使用我们的随机map ，我们只有一个函数可以转换一种方式，我们可以这样做，因为generate只是有一个输出值，而shrink需要一个值作为输入和输出。

好的，这样就不会飞了。 但是，我们还有另一种支持map的收缩相关类型。

树，树，树

为了封装收缩过程，我们引入了CandidateTree类，以及几个函数：

 @ dataclass ( frozen = True ) class CandidateTree ( Generic [ T ]): value : T candidates : Iterable [ CandidateTree [ T ]] def tree_from_shrink ( value : T , shrink : Shrink [ T ]) -> CandidateTree [ T ]: # implementation omitted def tree_map ( func : Callable [[ T ], U ], tree : CandidateTree [ T ]) -> CandidateTree [ U ]: # implementation omitted

等一下 - 我们可以将任何Shrink函数转换为CandidateTree ，我们可以为CandidateTree实现map 。 所以值得检查我们是否也可以实现mapN并为CandidateTree bind ，因为这正是Random支持的接口。 如果CandidateTree也支持它，那么我们可以打包Random和CandidateTree ，我们将拥有我们的集成 API。

我们将看到这确实是可能的。 据我所知， Reid Draper是第一个提出这个想法的人，并在 Clojure 测试库test.check的早期版本中实现了它。 事后看来似乎很明显！

那么让我们从mapN开始 - 为了清楚起见，我将在这里显示map2 。 带有 n 个参数的版本没有添加任何新内容，它只是更多的簿记。

我们先来想一想将2个CandidateTree map2在一起是什么意思。 这是我们需要实现的：

 def tree_map2 ( f : Callable [[ T , U ], V ], tree_1 : CandidateTree [ T ], tree_2 : CandidateTree [ U ], ) -> CandidateTree [ V ]: ...

像往常一样，看一个例子更容易。 这是分别并排收缩 1 和 3 的树。

考虑这一点的一个好方法是根据结果树，它是整数元组的树。 如果我们知道如何收缩元组的每个元素（我们会这样做——这正是tree_map2的两个CandidateTree参数），我们希望如何收缩 2 元组？

先缩小第一个元素，然后缩小第二个元素——两棵树的某种叉积，怎么样？ 生成的树如下所示：

这里发生了什么？ 树的根就是 (1,3)，它对应于参数树的根 1 和 3。 然后在组合树的根以下的第一层，我们有四个候选：

  • 第一个候选是tree_1的候选收缩（只有 0）与tree_2的根的组合。

  • 其余三个候选是 tree_2 的候选收缩与tree_2的根的tree_1 。

当我们沿着树向下移动时，这会递归地继续下去。 在代码中：

 def tree_map2 ( f : Callable [[ T , U ], V ], tree_1 : CandidateTree [ T ], tree_2 : CandidateTree [ U ], ) -> CandidateTree [ V ]: value = f ( tree_1 . value , tree_2 . value ) candidates_1 = ( tree_map2 ( f , candidate , tree_2 ) for candidate in tree_1 . candidates ) candidates_2 = ( tree_map2 ( f , tree_1 , candidate ) for candidate in tree_2 . candidates ) return CandidateTree ( value = value , candidates = itertools . chain ( candidates_1 , candidates_2 )) )

边栏：现在我们正在用树做更多有趣的事情，我不得不改变CandidateTree的定义，而不是上一篇文章中的定义。 特别是， candidates字段现在被迭代多次 - 例如在上面的tree_map2的定义中。 这意味着我们不能再只使用一个可迭代对象，因为一旦它被迭代，它就不能被重置。

 class CandidateTree ( Generic [ T ]): def __init__ ( self , value : T , candidates : Iterable [ CandidateTree [ T ]]) -> None : self . _value = value ( self . _candidates ,) = itertools . tee ( candidates , 1 ) @ property def value ( self ): return self . _value @ property def candidates ( self ): return copy ( self . _candidates )

实现细节并不重要，但对性能重要的是，首先， candidates者被延迟评估，其次是它们被缓存。 如果没有，我们要么做太多工作，要么一遍又一遍地重复同样的工作。 事实上，我最初的实现在self._candidates中使用了一个简单的list ，但它非常慢，以至于简单的测试需要几秒钟，而更多涉及的测试需要几分钟。

新定义使用了我在某处找到的一个小技巧：如果您使用itertools.tee ，您可以copy生成的可迭代对象（因此它从一开始就重新迭代），并且itertools.tee还增量缓存元素。 因此，在上面的代码中，您可以将itertools.tee读取为“返回缓存的迭代器”，并将其copy为“将迭代器重置为开始”。

让我们通过实现tree_bind来结束这个拥抱树的废话。 请记住，所有类绑定函数都有如下签名：

 def tree_bind ( f : Callable [[ T ], CandidateTree [ U ]], tree : CandidateTree [ T ] ) -> CandidateTree [ U ]: ...

这允许调用者注入一个任意的f ，它可以为输入树 tree 的每个元素返回一个全新的tree 。 这比tree_map更强大，因为 map 保持树的形状 - 不能添加或删除新节点。 但是，使用tree_bind ，生成的树可能看起来完全不同，具体取决于f所做的事情。

我只是想把实现抛给你看，然后用一个例子来讨论它。

 def tree_bind ( f : Callable [[ T ], CandidateTree [ U ]], tree : CandidateTree [ T ] ) -> CandidateTree [ U ]: tree_u = f ( tree . value ) candidates = ( tree_bind ( f , candidate ) for candidate in tree . candidates ) return CandidateTree ( value = tree_u . value , candidates = itertools . chain ( candidates , tree_u . candidates ) )

这实际上是自己写的——考虑到论点和它们的类型，很难想出其他任何东西（如果你不想学究气，就像那些在你问“你能不能打开灯”时回答“是”的人一样） ？”然后得意地指出他们已经回答了你的问题，而你给了他们精神中的中指）。

新树的根是对输入树应用f并取结果树的根value的结果。 为了获得子树（ candidates字段），我们在每个输入树的候选上递归调用tree_bind - 再次给我们一个树列表。 唯一要做的决定是如何结合tree_u.candidates candidate应该先去哪一个？ 我声称将candidates放在首位是最有意义的，因为这对应于“外部”树。

让我们通过一个例子来理解这个声明和实现。 这个例子是人为的，不是很实用，所以我不能围绕它编一个好故事——它让人想起缩小列表，我们首先要缩小列表的长度，然后是单个元素。

我们将从一个缩小整数 3 的树开始，就像我们之前已经看过几次一样。 这棵树的元素将确定由字母c组成的字符串的长度。 函数letters创建一个给定长度的CandidateTree ，它将length乘以字母c到b再到a 。 这是缩小整数 3 的树，以及由letters分别返回的长度值为 1、2 和 3 的树：

请注意，在缩小时， letters永远不会改变字符串的长度——它只是试图通过靠近a来使字母变小。

如果我们现在使用tree_bind如下：

 tree_list_length = tree_from_shrink ( 3 , shrink_int ( 0 , 3 )) tree_bound = tree_bind ( letters , tree_list_length )

然后tree_bound如下所示：

首先，根节点ccc是将letters应用于输入树的根的结果，因此length = 3 。 在向下的第一级， ccc的收缩候选者是cc ， c ， -表示空字符串， bbb最后是aaa 。 这种进展是我们选择首先尝试从“外部”树收缩的直接结果 - tree_list_length的收缩候选分别是 2、1 和 0，这导致cc 、 c和- 。 剩下的值bbb和aaa是直接收缩ccc的结果，见第一张图中的第四棵树。 这递归地继续。

您可能在生成的树中注意到的一件事是，几个部分本质上是空字符串，重复了很多次。 这是从哪里来的？ 如果letters被要求创建一个length = 0的树，那么它实际上会创建一个具有将值c缩小为b和a的形状的树，但是每个候选字母重复 0 次，因此成为空字符串。 我们可以通过对字母进行小的更改来避免这种情况，以在长度为 0 的情况下返回一个不收缩的常量树。

 def tree_constant ( value : T ) -> CandidateTree [ T ]: return CandidateTree ( value , tuple ()) def letters ( length : int ): if length == 0 : return tree_constant ( '' ) abc = tree_from_shrink ( ord ( 'c' ), shrink_int ( ord ( 'a' ), ord ( 'c' ))) abc_repeat = tree_map ( lambda o : chr ( o ) * length , abc ) return abc_repeat

然后我们得到更合理的：

乍一看，这似乎是一个微不足道的烦恼，但并非没有后果。 正如我们将回到的，这种潜在的低效率是我们为拥有一个集成的收缩 API 而付出的成本的一部分，尤其会影响绑定的使用。

树木已经够了——是时候享用美味的草莓小吃了。


照片由 Olga Kudriavtseva 在 Unsplash 上拍摄

将 Humpty-Dumpty 重新组合在一起

我们现在已经准备好把所有东西放在一起，这样我们就可以运行一些测试了。

我们将再次从字面上重用我们的Random和相关函数的实现 - 但是因为我们现在将拥有map 、 mapN和所有其余部分作为主要 API，所以我在“旧” Random特定函数前面加上了前缀random_ ，例如：

 def random_constant ( value : T ) -> Random [ T ]: return Random ( lambda : value )

在更现实的设置中， Random及其函数以及CandidateTree及其tree_something函数将位于一个单独的模块中。

排除了预备知识，本节的目标是重新创建Random API，即constant 、 int_between 、 map 、 mapN和bind ，但结果 API 可以随机生成值并缩小它们，所有这些都不需要用户以某种方式使这两者保持同步或重复代码。

让我们首先定义这个东西的类型，从现在开始我将称之为“生成器”：

 Gen = Random [ CandidateTree [ T ]]

我们之前使用这个想法来扩展我们的库，就像在上一篇文章中一样，我们不是随机生成值T而是生成整个T的树。 但是现在我们更早地这样做了，这将使一切看起来都很好地集成在一起。 此外，由于CandidateTree的惰性，我们实际上并没有为每个值创建完整的树，因为这会非常慢。

然后让我们从实现constant开始：

 def constant ( value : T ) -> Gen [ T ]: return random_constant ( tree_constant ( value ))

如果这不是一个令人满意的实现，我不知道是什么！ 我们只是为Random和CandidateTree组合了两个常量函数。 我们能在剩下的时间里保持这种状态吗？

 def map ( func : Callable [[ T ], U ], gen : Gen [ T ]) -> Gen [ U ]: return random_map ( lambda tree : tree_map ( func , tree ), gen )

至少对map来说是肯定的。 让我们试试int_between ：

 def int_between ( low : int , high : int ) -> Gen [ int ]: return random_map ( lambda v : tree_from_shrink ( v , shrink_int ( low , high )), random_int_between ( low , high ))

有点毛茸茸，但有原因。 首先， shrink_int需要两个额外的参数—— low和high ——这表明收缩候选者被允许存在的范围。 在我们上次的实现中，我们总是试图缩小到零，这是作弊（在我的辩护中，我隐藏得很好......）。 集成收缩和随机生成的主要优点之一是它让我现在没有出路。 这是shrink_int的新实现 - 它的复杂性与原始实现相似：

 def shrink_int ( low : int , high : int ) -> Shrink [ int ]: target = 0 if low > 0 : target = low if high < 0 : target = high def shrinker ( value : int ) -> Iterable [ int ]: if value == target : return half = ( value - target ) // 2 current = value - half while half != 0 and current != target : yield current half = ( current - target ) // 2 current = current - half yield target return shrinker

它和以前做的事情类似，除了它确保候选收缩在[low,high]区间内，并尝试在target的方向上收缩，无论是low还是high ，以最接近零的为准。

你现在可能已经知道了 - 这里是mapN ：

 def mapN ( f : Callable [..., T ], gens : Iterable [ Gen [ Any ]]) -> Gen [ T ]: return random_mapN ( lambda trees : tree_mapN ( f , trees ), gens )

和map一模一样！ 当一个计划结合在一起时，我喜欢它。

最后， bind 。 像往常一样，害群之马来统治他们（好吧，这些混合的比喻真的很混乱，对不起）

尝试与map相同的方法教会了我们一些东西：

 def bind ( func : Callable [[ T ], Gen [ U ]], gen : Gen [ T ]) -> Gen [ U ]: # DOES NOT WORK return random_bind ( lambda tree : tree_bind ( func , tree ), gen )

你能看出为什么吗？ 考虑func返回的类型，以及tree_bind期望的类型。

剧透警报： func返回Gen[U] ，扩展为Random[CandidateTree[U]] 。 但是tree_bind期望func只返回CandidateTree[U] ！ 那么我们如何从Random[CandidateTree]中获得CandidateTree呢？ 唯一的选择是生成一个：

 def bind ( func : Callable [[ T ], Gen [ U ]], gen : Gen [ T ]) -> Gen [ U ]: def inner_bind ( value : T ) -> CandidateTree [ U ]: random_tree = func ( value ) return random_tree . generate () return random_map ( lambda tree : tree_bind ( inner_bind , tree ), gen )

这实际上意味着如果人们使用bind ，收缩的质量可能不是很好，因为在收缩外部值时，我们会随机重新生成内部值。 内在价值可能是测试首先失败的原因，所以我们正在丢失有趣的信息。 这样做的效果是缩小效果不如预期。 我们很快就会在一个例子中看到这个问题。

抽象——当你看到它时你就会知道它

现在我们拥有了实现for_all并再次test的所有工具。 事实上，漂亮的是，带有集成收缩的for_all的实现与我们第一个实现中没有收缩的实现完全相同。 更好的是， test的实现与PBT #3中的实现相同！

 @ dataclass ( frozen = True ) class TestResult : is_success : bool arguments : tuple [ Any ,...] # This is the only change: # replacing Random with Gen. Property = Gen [ TestResult ] def for_all ( gen : Gen [ T ], property : Callable [[ T ], Union [ Property , bool ]]) -> Property : def property_wrapper ( value : T ) -> Property : outcome = property ( value ) if isinstance ( outcome , bool ): return constant ( TestResult ( is_success = outcome , arguments = ( value ,))) else : return map ( lambda inner_out : replace ( inner_out , arguments = ( value ,) + inner_out . arguments ), outcome ) return bind ( property_wrapper , gen ) def test ( property : Property ): def do_shrink ( tree : CandidateTree [ TestResult ]) -> None : for smaller in tree . candidates : if not smaller . value . is_success : # cool, found a smaller value that still fails - keep shrinking print ( f "Shrinking: found smaller arguments { smaller . value . arguments } " ) do_shrink ( smaller ) break else : print ( f "Shrinking: gave up at arguments { tree . value . arguments } " ) for test_number in range ( 100 ): result = property . generate () if not result . value . is_success : print ( f "Fail: at test { test_number } with arguments { result . value . arguments } ." ) do_shrink ( result ) return print ( "Success: 100 tests passed." )

我们现在可以编写一个与我们编写它的方式相同的list[Person]生成器而不收缩，它仍然会收缩！

 def list_of_gen ( gens : Iterable [ Gen [ Any ]]) -> Gen [ list [ Any ]]: return mapN ( lambda args : list ( args ), gens ) def list_of_length ( l : int , gen : Gen [ T ]) -> Gen [ list [ T ]]: gen_of_list = list_of_gen ([ gen ] * l ) return gen_of_list def list_of ( gen : Gen [ T ]) -> Gen [ list [ T ]]: length = int_between ( 0 , 10 ) return bind ( lambda l : list_of_length ( l , gen ), length ) ages = int_between ( 0 , 100 ) letters = map ( chr , int_between ( ord ( 'a' ), ord ( 'z' ))) simple_names = map ( "" . join , list_of_length ( 6 , letters )) persons = mapN ( lambda a : Person ( * a ), ( simple_names , ages )) lists_of_person = list_of ( persons )

最后让我们看看它的实际效果：

 prop_sort_by_age = for_all ( lists_of_person , lambda persons_in : is_valid ( persons_in , sort_by_age ( persons_in ))) > test ( prop_sort_by_age ) Success : 100 tests passed . prop_wrong_sort_by_age = for_all ( lists_of_person , lambda persons_in : is_valid ( persons_in , wrong_sort_by_age ( persons_in ))) > test ( prop_wrong_sort_by_age ) Fail : at test 0 with arguments ([ Person ( name = 'tnrzar' , age = 21 ), Person ( name = 'nwgurq' , age = 23 ), Person ( name = 'cbvinc' , age = 22 ), Person ( name = 'baouyp' , age = 40 )],). ( 127 lines skipped ) Shrinking : gave up at arguments ([ Person ( name = 'aaaaaa' , age = 0 ), Person ( name = 'aaaaaa' , age = 0 ), Person ( name = 'aabaaa' , age = 0 ), Person ( name = 'aaaaaa' , age = 1 )],)

太好了，所以它有效！ 有点儿？ 这里有一些东西要解压。

首先要注意的是，收缩需要更长的时间，最终的结果也没有以前那么好。 还记得我们在上一篇文章中的非集成（解体？）收缩实现，经过大约 15 个收缩步骤，我们最终得到：

 [ Person ( name = '' , age = 1 ), Person ( name = 'a' , age = 0 )]

在我们最终得到长度为 4 的最终列表之前，现在有 127 次收缩，而不是长度为 2。 这是因为自动的内置收缩不如我们之前明确编写的专用list收缩器。 自动收缩策略仅在我们明确结构时才知道收缩 - 在这种情况下，我们为列表选择了一个长度，然后生成一个具有该长度的随机人员列表。 所以自动收缩器所能做的就是尝试使长度更小，这实质上意味着在列表末尾切断元素。

您可能还记得，专门构建的shrink_list进行了二进制切割 - 尝试一半，然后是列表的另一半。 这具有三个重要的优点：

  1. 在上面的示例中，列表的前两个元素不是必需的，但自动收缩只尝试删除最后的元素，这使得测试成功。 自动收缩永远不会删除前两个元素。

  2. 正如我们在上面看到的， bind每次尝试缩小外部值时都别无选择，只能重新生成内部值。 具体来说，它从四个未通过测试的人的列表开始，下一个缩小候选者将是一个新的随机生成的包含三个元素的列表。 如果那个碰巧通过了测试，那么缩小尝试就不会成功。 当涉及bind时，收缩部分变得随机。

  3. 这个问题 - bind不能很好地收缩 - 在基于属性的测试世界中是一个众所周知的问题。 例如， 在 test.check 中对此进行了解释。 据我所知，没有一个很好的解决方案，但是在实践中可以很好地缓解这个问题：

    1. 对于列表等常见类型，完全避免在生成器中使用bind ，这些类型通常内置于核心 PBT 库中。 通常为集合和其他类型实现定制的收缩功能，其工作方式与shrink_list相同。

    2. 如果可以使用map或mapN ，则不鼓励用户使用bind 。

    3. 有时有一种工具允许用户通过手动实现收缩候选函数来覆盖或扩展收缩候选。 具有讽刺意味的是，我们必须重新引入一些我们刚刚跳过的东西来避免，但至少在用户必须使用绑定并需要良好的收缩候选的情况下，它至少让用户有一个出路。

    最后的观察是，在最初的收缩中， Person.name被缩减为空字符串或单个字母，而现在收缩后的名称是六个字母的字符串。 这其实很好！ 我们首先要求名称是六个字母的字符串，回想一下simple_names = map("".join, list_of_length(6, letters)) 。 现在这个不变量在生成和收缩过程中都得到了尊重。 之前，shrinker 不知道这个六个字母的限制。 在这种情况下，没有任何真正的不良影响，但实际上您可以想象任何少于 6 个字母的名称都会被验证和拒绝，这会将收缩过程置于与原始故障不同的代码路径上，从而掩盖原始漏洞。

    结论

    具有集成收缩功能的基于属性的测试库存在于相当多的语言中。 第一个实现它的是 Clojure 的test.check 。 从那时起， Hedgehog旗下的 Haskell、F#、R 和 Scala 实现了，可能还有其他我不知道的实现。 事后看来，这个想法是对原始 QuickCheck 缩小实现的相对简单的重构——我在上一篇文章中暗示答案已经在那里，但如果你没有看到它也不要难过。 多年来，我一直盯着原始的 QuickCheck 代码，但没有看到机会。

    尽管有上诉，我们还是讨论了一些重要的权衡和需要注意的问题。 除此之外，这些方法都是在诸如 Haskell 和 Clojure 之类的纯函数式语言或至少默认为纯函数式语言的上下文中开发的。 正如我们之前讨论过的那样，收缩本质上依赖于能够在生成收缩值时捕获它，然后重复使用该值作为种子来找到更小的值。 但是如果这个值实际上不是一个值，而是一个可变对象，并且在测试期间它发生了变异，那么收缩候选将没有任何意义，收缩过程会变得非常混乱。

    理所当然地，人们需要为 Python 这样的命令式语言编写一个基于属性的测试库，才能真正考虑到这一点并想出一个替代解决方案。 再一次，从某种意义上说，答案在所有这些帖子中一直伴随着我们，但被很好地隐藏了。 神秘多吗？ 我们将在下一篇文章中深入探讨假设，敬请期待。

    像往常一样，在下面发表评论或在 Twitter @kurt2001上讨论。

    直到下一次！



原文:

This is the fourth post in a series about property-based testing. It describes how to restructure the original QuickCheck API so random generation and shrinking are unified. The first and introductory post in the series is What is it anyway?, followed by The Essentials of Vintage QuickCheck and Shrinking Take One.

The complete code for this post can be found on GitHub - in particular example.py and integrated.py.

In the last post we added shrinking to our property-based testing library. This worked well, but we touched on a few not so ideal aspects - in particular that we now need the user to implement and pass in a shrink function to for_all. Besides being an extra burden on the user, it can also lead to some confusing behavior if the generation and shrinking functions are not aligned.


Some stones that are nicely aligned, as opposed to random generation and shrinking. Photo by Riccardo Ginevri on Unsplash

Let's look at a contrived example:

prop_weird_shrink = for_all( int_between(-20, -1), shrink_int, lambda i: i * i < 0
)

We're generating strictly negative numbers, and check that the product of negative numbers is a positive number, but have flipped the greater than sign by mistake.

We easily find the bug, but during shrinking a weird thing happens:

> test(prop_weird_shrink)
Fail: at test 0 with arguments (-20,).
Shrinking: found smaller arguments (0,)
Shrinking: gave up - smallest arguments found (0,)

We report 0 as the smallest element that fails, but 0 wasn't even a possible generated value to begin with! In other words the shrink candidates don't satisfy the same invariants as the random generator.

Can we refactor our API so that it look and feels like the random generation API but can still shrink? I.e. can our users just write:

prop_weird_shrink = for_all( int_between(-20, -1), lambda i: i * i < 0
)

and get shrinking for free? And of course while automatically satisfying the same invariants as random generation.

The answer turns out to be yes, we can. Let’s see how.

Isn't it easy?

It's worth explaining briefly why we can't just pack up the interfaces for random generation and shrinking, implement the primitive functions on them and call it a day:

class Arbitrary(Generic[Value]): def generate(self) -> Value: ... def shrink(self, value: Value) -> Iterable[Value]: ...

This is pretty straightforward to implement for int_between for example - we already have random generation and we can pretty easily tweak shrink_int to take low, high arguments and make sure the smaller candidates lie in that range. The real problem occurs when we try to implement map for shrink. We need to support map because we wanted the same API for random+shrink as for random, and random supports map.

We'd like to have a function with this signature:

def shrink_map(f: Callable[[T],U], s: Shrink[T]) -> Shrink[U]: def shrinker(value: U) -> Iterable[U]: ... return shrinker

And remember that Shrink[T] just means a function that takes a T and returns an Iterable[T] of smaller values. So the inner shrinker function is the result of shrink_map. Now how to implement it? We could apply f to each candidate generated by the input shrinker s:

def shrink_map(f: Callable[[T],U], s: Shrink[T]) -> Shrink[U]: def shrinker(value: U) -> Iterable[U]: for candidate in s(...): yield f(candidate) return shrinker

That would definitely give us a bunch of U candidate values. But the problem is s needs some value T to produce its candidates, and we don't have it! We only have a value U. To drive this point home, here's an example with concrete types - say I want to turn shrink_int into shrink_char - there is, after all, a map of ASCII code integers to characters. Easy, you say, just convert the character to its ASCII code integer using ord, and back to its character using chr:

def make_shrink_char(shrink_int: Shrink[int]) -> Shrink[str]: def shrinker(value: str) -> Iterable[str]: for candidate in shrink_int(ord(value)): yield chr(candidate) return shrinker

But this is not a map - in fact to convert a shrink_int into a shrink_char we need two functions: one to convert integers to characters and one to convert characters to integers. With our random map we only have one function to convert one way, and we could do this because generate just has a value it outputs, while shrink needs a value as both input and output.

Ok, so that won't fly. However, we have another shrinking related type which does support map.

Trees, trees, trees

To encapsulate the shrinking process, we introduced the CandidateTree class, along with a couple of functions:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CandidateTree(Generic[T]): value: T candidates: Iterable[CandidateTree[T]] def tree_from_shrink( value: T, shrink: Shrink[T]) -> CandidateTree[T]: # implementation omitted

def tree_map( func: Callable[[T], U], tree: CandidateTree[T]) -> CandidateTree[U]: # implementation omitted

Hold on a minute - we can turn any Shrink function into a CandidateTree, and we can implement map for CandidateTree. So it's worth checking if we can also implement mapN and bind for CandidateTree, because that's exactly the interface Random supports. If CandidateTree also supports it, then we could pack up Random and CandidateTree and we'd have our integrated API.

We'll see that this is indeed possible. To the best of my knowledge, Reid Draper was the first to come up with this, and implemented it in an early version of the Clojure testing library test.check. Seems obvious only in hindsight!

Let's start with mapN then - for clarity I'll show map2 here. The version with n arguments doesn't add anything new, it's just more bookkeeping.

Let's first think about what it means to map2 CandidateTrees together. Here's what we need to implement:

def tree_map2( f: Callable[[T, U], V], tree_1: CandidateTree[T], tree_2: CandidateTree[U],
) -> CandidateTree[V]: ...

As usual, it's easier to look at an example. Here's the trees that shrink 1 and 3 respectively side by side.

What would it mean to tree_map2 over those two trees, with f just making a tuple?

tree_1 = tree_from_shrink(1, shrink_int(0,10))
tree_2 = tree_from_shrink(3, shrink_int(0,10))
tree_tuples = tree_map2( lambda x,y: (x,y), tree_1, tree_2)

A nice way to think about this is in terms of the resulting tree, which is a tree of tuples of integers. If we know how to shrink each element of a tuple (which we do - those are exactly the two CandidateTree arguments to tree_map2), how would we like to shrink the 2-tuple?

How about shrinking the first element first, then the second element - some kind of cross-product of the two trees? The resulting tree looks like:

What's going on here? The root of the tree is just (1,3) which corresponds to the roots 1 and 3 of the argument trees. Then the first level below the root of the combined tree, we have four candidates:

  • the first candidate is a combination of the candidate shrink of tree_1 (just 0) with the root of tree_2.

  • the remaining three candidates are a combination of the candidate shrinks of tree_2 with the root of tree_1.

And this continues recursively as we move down the tree. In code:

def tree_map2( f: Callable[[T, U], V], tree_1: CandidateTree[T], tree_2: CandidateTree[U],
) -> CandidateTree[V]: value = f(tree_1.value, tree_2.value) candidates_1 = ( tree_map2(f, candidate, tree_2) for candidate in tree_1.candidates ) candidates_2 = ( tree_map2(f, tree_1, candidate) for candidate in tree_2.candidates ) return CandidateTree( value = value, candidates = itertools.chain( candidates_1, candidates_2 )) )

Sidebar: now that we're doing more interesting things with trees, I've had to change the definition of CandidateTree vs the one in the last post. In particular, the candidates field is now iterated over multiple times - for example in the definition of tree_map2 above. This means we can't just use an iterable anymore, because once that's iterated over, it can't be reset.

class CandidateTree(Generic[T]): def __init__ (self, value: T, candidates: Iterable[CandidateTree[T]]) -> None: self._value = value (self._candidates,) = itertools.tee(candidates, 1) @property def value(self): return self._value @property def candidates(self): return copy(self._candidates)

The implementation details are not important, but what is important for performance is that first, the candidates are evaluated lazily, and second that they are cached. If not, we are either doing too much work, or repeating the same work over and over again. In fact my original implementation used a simple list in self._candidates but it was extremely slow, to the point where simple tests took several seconds and more involved tests took several minutes.

The new definition uses a little trick I picked up somewhere: if you use itertools.tee you can copy the resulting iterable (so it's re-iterated from the start), and itertools.tee also incrementally caches the elements. So in the above code you can read itertools.tee as "return a cached iterator", and copy as "reset the iterator to the start".

Let's wrap up this treehugging nonsense by implementing tree_bind. Remember all bind-like functions have a signature like:

def tree_bind( f: Callable[[T], CandidateTree[U]], tree: CandidateTree[T]
) -> CandidateTree[U]: ...

Which allows the caller to inject an arbitrary f that can return an entirely new tree for each element of the input tree tree. This is more powerful than tree_map because map maintains the shape of the tree - no new nodes can be added or removed. tree_map2 also restricts how the shape of the input trees are used - it can only produce a "product" of the input trees. With tree_bind however, the resulting tree can look entirely different, depending on the what f does.

I'm just going to chuck the implementation at you, and then discuss it with an example.

def tree_bind( f: Callable[[T], CandidateTree[U]], tree: CandidateTree[T]
) -> CandidateTree[U]: tree_u = f(tree.value) candidates = ( tree_bind(f, candidate) for candidate in tree.candidates ) return CandidateTree( value = tree_u.value, candidates = itertools.chain( candidates, tree_u.candidates ) )

This practically writes itself - given the arguments and their types, it's pretty hard to come up with anything else (if you aren't trying to be pedantic, like those people who go "yes" when you ask "can you turn on the light?" and then smugly point out they've answered your question while you give them the mental middle finger).

The root of the new tree is the result of applying f to the input tree, and taking the root value of the resulting tree. To get the subtrees (the candidates field) we recursively call tree_bind on each of input tree's candidates - giving us a list of trees again. The only decision to make is how to combine candidateand tree_u.candidates: which one should go first? I'm claiming it makes most sense to put candidates first, because that corresponds to the "outer" tree.

Let's make sense of that statement, and the implementation, by looking at an example. The example is contrived and not very practical, so I can't spin a nice story around it - it's reminiscent of shrinking a list, where we first want to shrink the length of the list, and then the individual elements.

We'll start with a tree that shrinks the integer 3, like we've seen a few times before already. The elements of this tree will determine the length of a string consisting of the letter c. The function letters creates a CandidateTree for a given length, that shrinks length times the letter c to b and then to a. letters is the function we intend to use as the f argument to tree_bind:

def letters(length: int): abc = tree_from_shrink(ord('c'), shrink_int(ord('a'), ord('c'))) abc_repeat = tree_map(lambda o: chr(o) * length, abc) return abc_repeat

Here's the tree that shrinks the integer 3, and the trees returned by letters for length values 1, 2 and 3 respectively:

Note while shrinking, letters never changes the length of the string - it only tries to make the letters smaller by moving closer to a.

If we now use tree_bind as follows:

tree_list_length = tree_from_shrink(3, shrink_int(0,3))
tree_bound = tree_bind(letters, tree_list_length)

Then tree_bound looks as follows:
